title: quassia subcymosa
intro: Quassia subcymosa, also known as yellow quassia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's bark and roots have been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include being a natural insecticide and antimicrobial agent. The health benefits of Quassia subcymosa include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve stomach issues, and support the immune system, making it a valuable treatment for various gastrointestinal problems. Herbal preparations of Quassia subcymosa typically involve decoctions or infusions of the bark or roots, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from indigestion and diarrhea to fever and skin irritations.
